Meaning: Ever dropped out of high school

EDA Note: Strong academic performance signal and one of the most important candidate predictors.

Meaning: How sure student is they will receive diploma
EDA Note: Very important graduation-confidence feature; strongly tied to dropout risk.

Meaning: Highest education level student believes they meet requirements for
EDA Note: Strong academic readiness / eligibility feature with clear modeling value.
Strong educational expectation feature; retained after removing duplicate S2EDUEXP.
Meaningful academic performance feature; ordinal direction must be interpreted carefully.
Behavior / motivation feature; may show nonlinear predictive value beyond simple correlation.
Socioeconomic risk indicator; important contextual predictor.
Parent educational expectation; useful contextual predictor that may complement student expectation.

Meaning: Belief that people can do okay even if they drop out
EDA Note: Attitude toward dropout; likely useful as a supporting belief-based feature.

Meaning: Belief that studying in high school pays off later
EDA Note: Attitude-based feature; may add supporting signal rather than being a dominant predictor alone.
Continuous self-efficacy feature; likely useful but weaker than direct academic performance measures.
Continuous science self-efficacy feature; conceptually valuable and may complement math self-efficacy.
Earlier grade-level feature; useful for progression context but may be weaker than later academic indicators.
Later grade-level feature; likely more directly related to dropout risk than prior grade status.
